summary: Phase 2 Reports hardening — narrow verification. Backend: 14/14 framework pytests + 7/7 new Phase 2 E2E tests pass. PDF native /Encrypt encryption confirmed, ZIP AES-256 for CSV/XLSX confirmed, `reason` field accepted and persisted, `protection_kind` surfaced in /api/exports/{id}, PHI 403 gate intact, no plaintext `password_plain` values in DB, `password_enc` stored with 'enc:' prefix. Frontend: CRITICAL WIRING BUG — the export dropdown bypasses the new PhiConsentDialog for PHI reports; export fires immediately and surfaces the 403 error in the result dialog. Non-PHI flow works (straight to result dialog).

minor.issue: 5 legacy rows still carry a null `password_plain` field (created 2026-04-21T17:05..17:16). No plaintext leakage — all values are None — but the field should not exist per the new invariant. One-off `$unset:{password_plain:''}` migration recommended.

ui_bugs.issue: DropdownMenuItem onClick is wired to handleExport(fmt) directly (line 379). It must call requestExportFormat(fmt) so the PhiConsentDialog gate runs for PHI reports. Result: on PHI reports (e.g. /reports/appointments_list) clicking Export → CSV/PDF/EXCEL bypasses the consent dialog entirely, submits the export immediately, and shows the 403 'Exports of PHI reports require reporting.export_phi permission.' in the result dialog. `phi-consent-dialog`, `phi-consent-reason`, `phi-consent-cancel`, `phi-consent-confirm` are reachable in code but never rendered via the main Export button. Reproduced on Appointments report with admin login.

integration_issues.issue: Because the consent dialog never opens, the UX contract for the Phase 2 feature is broken — `reason` is never captured from the UI and AES-256 ZIP / native PDF copy is never shown to the user. Backend happily accepts `reason` when supplied; the issue is 100% client-side wiring.

action_items: [CRITICAL] In /app/frontend/src/pages/reports/ReportViewer.jsx line 379, change `onClick={() => handleExport(fmt)}` to `onClick={() => requestExportFormat(fmt)}` so PHI reports open the PhiConsentDialog before firing the export.
[Minor] Ship a one-off migration to $unset the legacy null `password_plain` field from the 5 existing exports rows so the strict 'field must not exist' invariant holds everywhere.
critical_code_review_comments: export_writer.py _write_pdf hardcodes `canPrint=1, canCopy=1, canAnnotate=0, canModify=0` — copying is still allowed which is surprising for a 'HIPAA-grade' export; confirm with product whether copy should also be blocked.

services/exports/__init__.py get_export reveals the one-time password on the FIRST poll by the original actor and then $unsets password_enc. This means if the requester's first poll hits while status is still pending, one_time_password is never surfaced. Current guard requires status=='ready' so that is fine — but a subsequent poll after the first successful reveal will correctly return one_time_password=null because password_enc is absent. Double-checked: behaviour matches the invariant.
create_report_export writes password_enc:None on non-PHI exports — no issue, but worth storing only when truthy to keep rows lean.

success_rate.frontend: 50% (non-PHI export dialog OK; PHI consent dialog dead)

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 (reauth required — UI defaults to PIN, click 'Use password instead')
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_ denials_log CSV/PDF export rows created for Phase 2 assertions (harmless; non-PHI, no PHI leakage; will self-expire via EXPORT_TTL).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes ReportViewer.jsx:379 to call requestExportFormat(fmt), re-run this Playwright flow: login → /reports/appointments_list → reauth (password) → Export → CSV → expect phi-consent-dialog visible with 'AES-256' copy, phi-consent-reason input, phi-consent-cancel, phi-consent-confirm. Then PDF → expect 'natively encrypted' copy. Then /reports/denials_log → Export → CSV must skip consent dialog and go straight to result dialog. Backend pytest regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_reports_framework.py /app/backend/tests/test_reports_phase2_e2e.py -v` — both suites are 21/21 green on this iteration.
rca of the issue: The Export dropdown (ReportViewer.jsx:376-388) was refactored in Phase 2 to gate PHI exports behind a new PhiConsentDialog. A helper `requestExportFormat(format)` was added (line 238) that checks meta.contains_phi and either opens the dialog or runs handleExport directly. However the DropdownMenuItem.onClick was left wired to the OLD handler `handleExport(fmt)` on line 379, so the helper is never called from the main UI path — the consent dialog is effectively dead. Mitigation: one-line change `onClick={() => requestExportFormat(fmt)}` reinstates the intended flow.
